ontology.name: Pain Management Clinical Visit Record Ontology
ontology.description: Models the structure of pain management and rehabilitation follow-up visit records, including patient demographics, injury/claim context, clinical findings, diagnostics, and treatment recommendations. Used to generate structured clinical documentation for workers' compensation and musculoskeletal pain cases.

nodes.label: Clinical Visit Record
nodes.description: The top-level document representing a single patient encounter at a pain care facility
nodes.semantics: Each instance is tied to a specific patient, provider, date, and visit type

nodes.label: Medical Practice
nodes.description: The clinical facility or practice where the visit occurs, including name, locations, and contact information
nodes.semantics: Varies by practice name, number of locations, and specialty focus

nodes.label: Practice Name
nodes.description: The formal name of the medical practice or rehabilitation center
nodes.semantics: Fixed per document instance, appears in header of every page

nodes.label: Practice Location
nodes.description: Physical address and telephone number of the practice; may include multiple locations
nodes.semantics: One or more address/phone pairs; varies per practice

nodes.label: Provider
nodes.description: The licensed physician or clinician authoring or signing the visit record
nodes.semantics: Name, credentials, and signature vary; may include attending and co-signing providers

nodes.label: Provider Credentials
nodes.description: Professional degree and specialty designation of the provider
nodes.semantics: Varies by provider; drives authority and scope of recommendations

nodes.label: Patient
nodes.description: The individual receiving care, characterized by demographics and identifying information
nodes.semantics: Demographics (name, DOB, age, gender, ID) vary per document instance

nodes.label: Patient Demographics
nodes.description: Core identifying attributes of the patient including name, date of birth, age, gender, and patient ID
nodes.semantics: All vary per patient; gender drives pronoun use throughout narrative

nodes.label: Visit Metadata
nodes.description: Administrative details about the specific clinical encounter including date and visit type
nodes.semantics: Date and visit type vary per encounter; visit type shapes which sections are included

nodes.label: Visit Type
nodes.description: Classification of the encounter that drives the scope and structure of the clinical note
nodes.semantics: Determines which exam sections are required and the depth of history taking

nodes.label: Claim Information
nodes.description: Legal and administrative information about the injury claim context
nodes.semantics: Claim type and causal relationship vary; shapes work status and impairment sections

nodes.label: Claim Type
nodes.description: The legal classification of the injury or illness claim
nodes.semantics: Drives causal relationship language, impairment ratings, and work status documentation

nodes.label: Causal Relationship
nodes.description: Attribution of the injury or condition to a specific cause
nodes.semantics: Varies based on claim type and accident details

nodes.label: Work Status
nodes.description: Documentation of the patient's current and historical ability to work due to the injury
nodes.semantics: Includes missed work dates, current work capacity, and return-to-work assessment
examples: Patient currently not working, first missed work 7/8/2021
Full Work, Limited Work, No Work

nodes.label: Temporary Impairment Percentage
nodes.description: Clinician-assigned percentage of functional impairment due to the injury
nodes.semantics: Varies from 0-100%; used for workers' compensation documentation

nodes.label: Chief Complaint
nodes.description: The primary symptoms or pain regions reported by the patient at the visit
nodes.semantics: Body region(s) and pain type vary; drives which physical exam sections are performed
examples: neck pain, low back pain, bilateral knee pain, bilateral shoulder pain

nodes.label: Body Region
nodes.description: Anatomical area of complaint or examination; drives the structure of the physical exam
nodes.semantics: One or more regions per visit; each region has its own exam subsection

nodes.label: History of Present Illness (HPI)
nodes.description: Narrative description of the patient's symptoms, progression, prior treatments, and functional limitations
nodes.semantics: Length and detail vary with case complexity; includes pain ratings, prior interventions, and ADL impact
examples: Patient continues to have back pain, grades pain 9/10, denies radiating pain
Patient reports neck pain 5/10 with dull to sharp quality

nodes.label: Pain Rating
nodes.description: Numeric pain scale score reported by the patient, typically 0-10
nodes.semantics: Varies per body region and visit; used to track progress over time

nodes.label: Prior Treatment
nodes.description: Treatments the patient has previously received or is currently undergoing
nodes.semantics: Varies by case; includes procedures, therapies, medications, and surgeries

nodes.label: Allergies
nodes.description: Documented drug or substance allergies including reaction type
nodes.semantics: Varies per patient; presence or absence must be documented

nodes.label: Medications
nodes.description: All medications prescribed or currently taken by the patient, including dosage, frequency, and duration
nodes.semantics: Divided into today's prescriptions and current ongoing medications; varies by case complexity

nodes.label: Medication Details
nodes.description: Specific attributes of each prescribed medication: name, formulation, dose, route, frequency, and duration
nodes.semantics: Each medication entry must include all sub-attributes; duration typically in days

nodes.label: Past Medical History
nodes.description: Documented pre-existing medical conditions organized by body system
nodes.semantics: Categorized by system (cardiovascular, musculoskeletal, neurological, endocrine, oncological); varies by patient

nodes.label: Surgical History
nodes.description: Prior surgical procedures undergone by the patient
nodes.semantics: Varies per patient; relevant surgeries may be referenced in HPI and recommendations

nodes.label: Social History
nodes.description: Patient lifestyle factors including tobacco and alcohol use
nodes.semantics: Tobacco and alcohol status vary; may include occupation and activity level

nodes.label: Vital Signs
nodes.description: Objective biometric measurements recorded at the visit
nodes.semantics: Height, weight, BMI, BSA, and blood pressure vary per visit; date/time and laterality of BP documented

nodes.label: Review of Systems (ROS)
nodes.description: Systematic patient-reported symptom review across all major body systems
nodes.semantics: Each system listed with admits/denies for each symptom; systems covered vary by visit
examples: Musculoskeletal: Admits to neck pain, low back pain

nodes.label: Physical Examination
nodes.description: Objective clinical findings organized by body region and exam component
nodes.semantics: Modular structure with one subsection per body region; components include inspection, palpation, ROM, strength, sensation, reflexes, special tests

nodes.label: Range of Motion
nodes.description: Measured degrees of movement in each direction for a given body region, compared to previous visit and normal range
nodes.semantics: Tabular format with flexion, extension, rotation, side-bending; previous vs. current vs. normal columns

nodes.label: Muscle Strength
nodes.description: Graded muscle strength testing (0-5 scale) for specific muscle groups in a body region
nodes.semantics: Tabular with previous, current, and normal columns; laterality specified

nodes.label: Special Orthopedic Tests
nodes.description: Named orthopedic or neurological provocation tests with positive/negative/equivocal results and laterality
nodes.semantics: Set of tests varies by body region; results drive diagnostic and treatment decisions

nodes.label: Sensation Testing
nodes.description: Assessment of sensory function at specified anatomical landmarks
nodes.semantics: Varies by body region; typically normal or abnormal with specific dermatomal locations

nodes.label: Muscle Stretch Reflexes
nodes.description: Deep tendon reflex findings at specific anatomical locations, graded on standard scale
nodes.semantics: Varies by body region examined; graded 0-4+ with laterality noted

nodes.label: Diagnostic Tests
nodes.description: Imaging studies, laboratory tests, or other diagnostic evaluations with dates, technique, findings, and impressions
nodes.semantics: Varies by test type (MRI, X-ray, CT, EMG); includes comparison to prior studies

nodes.label: Imaging Findings
nodes.description: Detailed radiologic observations at each spinal level or anatomical region with impression summary
nodes.semantics: Level-by-level findings for spine; includes severity grading and comparison to prior
examples: L4-5: Anterolisthesis, severe bilateral facet arthropathy, moderate to severe canal stenosis
Grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5

nodes.label: Assessment / Diagnoses
nodes.description: Formal diagnostic codes and labels representing the clinician's conclusions from the visit
nodes.semantics: ICD-10 coded diagnoses; multiple diagnoses per visit organized by body region
examples: M17.2 Bilateral Post-Traumatic Osteoarthritis of Knee

nodes.label: ICD-10 Diagnosis Code
nodes.description: Standardized alphanumeric code classifying each diagnosed condition
nodes.semantics: Each diagnosis carries a code and description; drives billing and treatment justification

nodes.label: Recommendations / Treatment Plan
nodes.description: Ordered list of clinical recommendations including therapies, procedures, medications, and justifications
nodes.semantics: Numbered items; each recommendation includes clinical rationale referencing exam findings and failed prior treatments

nodes.label: Treatment Justification
nodes.description: Clinical rationale documenting why a recommended treatment is medically necessary, referencing failed conservative treatments and objective findings
nodes.semantics: Required for procedures and injections; references duration of prior treatment, percent pain relief achieved, and diagnostic findings
examples: Patient underwent 3 months of PT with less than 15% pain relief
Intra-articular injection with less than 20% relief, step-therapy failed

nodes.label: Functional Goals
nodes.description: Short-term and long-term measurable goals for the patient's recovery and functional improvement
nodes.semantics: Quantified where possible (blocks walked, degrees of ROM, ADL performance); short-term vs. long-term framing
examples: Improve ADL performance including dressing and bathing

nodes.label: ADL Impact
nodes.description: Assessment of how the patient's condition affects their ability to perform activities of daily living
nodes.semantics: Specific ADLs named; severity of limitation documented in HPI and recommendations

nodes.label: Signature Block
nodes.description: Attestation section including provider signature, date, and co-signing provider if applicable
nodes.semantics: Required at end of document; may include multiple providers

nodes.label: Radiology Report
nodes.description: Appended or referenced formal imaging report including technique, clinical history, findings, impression, and radiologist signature
nodes.semantics: Separate sub-document appended to visit record; includes ordering and reading provider information

nodes.label: Radiologist
nodes.description: The physician who interpreted the imaging study, including name, credentials, and signature date
nodes.semantics: Varies per study; distinct from treating provider

pipeline.promptReady:
# Pain Management Clinical Visit Record Ontology

Models the structure of pain management and rehabilitation follow-up visit records, including patient demographics, injury/claim context, clinical findings, diagnostics, and treatment recommendations. Used to generate structured clinical documentation for workers' compensation and musculoskeletal pain cases.

**Domain:** healthcare

## Classes

### Clinical Visit Record
The top-level document representing a single patient encounter at a pain care facility

**Properties:**
- Visit Metadata: Administrative details about the specific clinical encounter including date and visit type
  Examples: Date of Visit: 5/14/2024, Follow-Up Exam, Date of Visit: 3/10/2023, Initial Evaluation
- Claim Information: Legal and administrative information about the injury claim context
  Examples: Type of Claim: WC, Type of Claim: No-Fault, Type of Claim: General Liability
- Chief Complaint: The primary symptoms or pain regions reported by the patient at the visit
  Examples: neck pain, low back pain, bilateral knee pain, bilateral shoulder pain, lower back pain with radiculopathy

**Relations:**
- occurs at → Medical Practice
- authored by → Provider
- documents encounter with → Patient
- contains HPI → History of Present Illness (HPI)
- documents allergies → Allergies
- documents medications → Medications
- records PMH → Past Medical History
- records surgical history → Surgical History
- records social history → Social History
- includes vitals → Vital Signs
- includes ROS → Review of Systems (ROS)
- includes physical exam → Physical Examination
- references diagnostics → Diagnostic Tests
- concludes with → Assessment / Diagnoses
- concludes with → Recommendations / Treatment Plan
- attested by → Signature Block
- appends → Radiology Report

**Examples:**
- Follow-Up Exam
- Initial Consultation
- Post-Procedure Visit

### Medical Practice
The clinical facility or practice where the visit occurs, including name, locations, and contact information

**Properties:**
- Practice Name: The formal name of the medical practice or rehabilitation center
  Examples: Northshore Pain & Rehabilitation, Tristate Spine Center
- Practice Location: Physical address and telephone number of the practice; may include multiple locations
  Examples: 200 Madison Avenue, Suite 401, Hempstead, NY 11550, 450 Garden Boulevard, Riverside, NY 11420

**Examples:**
- Northshore Pain & Rehabilitation
- Orthopedic Associates

### Provider
The licensed physician or clinician authoring or signing the visit record

**Properties:**
- Provider Credentials: Professional degree and specialty designation of the provider
  Examples: M.D., D.O., D.C.

**Examples:**
- James Carter, M.D.
- Marcus Reid, D.O.

### Patient
The individual receiving care, characterized by demographics and identifying information

**Properties:**
- Patient Demographics: Core identifying attributes of the patient including name, date of birth, age, gender, and patient ID
  Examples: Age: 34, Gender: Female, Age: 52, Gender: Male

**Examples:**
- Maria Rivera, DOB 04/22/1988
- Daniel Ortiz, DOB 11/05/1972

### Visit Metadata
Administrative details about the specific clinical encounter including date and visit type

**Examples:**
- Date of Visit: 5/14/2024, Follow-Up Exam
- Date of Visit: 3/10/2023, Initial Evaluation

### Claim Information
Legal and administrative information about the injury claim context

**Properties:**
- Causal Relationship: Attribution of the injury or condition to a specific cause
  Examples: Work related accident, Motor vehicle accident, Slip and fall
- Work Status: Documentation of the patient's current and historical ability to work due to the injury
  Examples: Patient currently not working, first missed work 7/8/2021, Full Work, Limited Work, No Work
- Temporary Impairment Percentage: Clinician-assigned percentage of functional impairment due to the injury
  Examples: 100%, 75%, 50%

**Examples:**
- Type of Claim: WC
- Type of Claim: No-Fault
- Type of Claim: General Liability

### Work Status
Documentation of the patient's current and historical ability to work due to the injury

**Examples:**
- Patient currently not working, first missed work 7/8/2021
- Full Work, Limited Work, No Work

### Chief Complaint
The primary symptoms or pain regions reported by the patient at the visit

**Examples:**
- neck pain, low back pain, bilateral knee pain, bilateral shoulder pain
- lower back pain with radiculopathy

### History of Present Illness (HPI)
Narrative description of the patient's symptoms, progression, prior treatments, and functional limitations

**Properties:**
- Pain Rating: Numeric pain scale score reported by the patient, typically 0-10
  Examples: 9/10, 7/10, 4/10, 5/10
- Prior Treatment: Treatments the patient has previously received or is currently undergoing
  Examples: epidural steroid injection, left total knee replacement, acupuncture, physical therapy
- ADL Impact: Assessment of how the patient's condition affects their ability to perform activities of daily living
  Examples: difficulty bathing, dressing, grooming, unable to cook, difficulty walking more than 2 blocks

**Examples:**
- Patient continues to have back pain, grades pain 9/10, denies radiating pain
- Patient reports neck pain 5/10 with dull to sharp quality

### Allergies
Documented drug or substance allergies including reaction type

**Examples:**
- Ceclor: itching
- gabapentin: itching
- NKDA

### Medications
All medications prescribed or currently taken by the patient, including dosage, frequency, and duration

**Properties:**
- Medication Details: Specific attributes of each prescribed medication: name, formulation, dose, route, frequency, and duration
  Examples: 1 tablet twice daily for 30 days, 1 application every 8 hours for 30 days

**Examples:**
- Percocet 5-325mg twice daily
- cyclobenzaprine ER 15mg daily
- diclofenac topical gel 3%

### Past Medical History
Documented pre-existing medical conditions organized by body system

**Examples:**
- Hypertension
- Osteoporosis
- Stroke
- Kidney problems
- Cancer

### Surgical History
Prior surgical procedures undergone by the patient

**Examples:**
- appendectomy
- bariatric sleeve surgery
- left total knee replacement

### Social History
Patient lifestyle factors including tobacco and alcohol use

**Examples:**
- current smoker, every day
- social drinking
- non-smoker

### Vital Signs
Objective biometric measurements recorded at the visit

**Examples:**
- Height: 67 in, Weight: 217 lbs, BMI: 34.0
- BP: 128/72 Left Arm Sitting

### Review of Systems (ROS)
Systematic patient-reported symptom review across all major body systems

**Examples:**
- Constitutional: Denies fever, chills
- Musculoskeletal: Admits to neck pain, low back pain

### Physical Examination
Objective clinical findings organized by body region and exam component

**Properties:**
- Range of Motion: Measured degrees of movement in each direction for a given body region, compared to previous visit and normal range
  Examples: Lumbar Flexion: 55° (Normal 90°), Cervical Rotation Left: 65°, Right: 55°
- Muscle Strength: Graded muscle strength testing (0-5 scale) for specific muscle groups in a body region
  Examples: Hip Flexion: 4/5 (Normal 5/5), Shoulder Abduction: 4/5
- Special Orthopedic Tests: Named orthopedic or neurological provocation tests with positive/negative/equivocal results and laterality
  Examples: SLR positive bilateral, McMurray's test positive, Neer's test positive, Spurling's test equivocal
- Sensation Testing: Assessment of sensory function at specified anatomical landmarks
  Examples: Dorsal foot (1st Web): Normal, Medial forearm/elbow: Normal, Digit 3 (Middle Finger): Normal
- Muscle Stretch Reflexes: Deep tendon reflex findings at specific anatomical locations, graded on standard scale
  Examples: Biceps 2+, Patellar reflex 2+ symmetrical, Brachioradialis 2+

**Relations:**
- organized by → Body Region

**Examples:**
- Exam Lumbar Spine
- Exam Cervical Spine
- Examination Knee Bilateral
- Examination Shoulder Bilateral

### Diagnostic Tests
Imaging studies, laboratory tests, or other diagnostic evaluations with dates, technique, findings, and impressions

**Properties:**
- Imaging Findings: Detailed radiologic observations at each spinal level or anatomical region with impression summary
  Examples: L4-5: Anterolisthesis, severe bilateral facet arthropathy, moderate to severe canal stenosis, Grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5

**Examples:**
- MRI Lumbar Spine Non-Contrast 2/9/2024
- X-ray bilateral knees
- EMG/NCS

### Assessment / Diagnoses
Formal diagnostic codes and labels representing the clinician's conclusions from the visit

**Properties:**
- ICD-10 Diagnosis Code: Standardized alphanumeric code classifying each diagnosed condition
  Examples: M54.59, M54.12, M23.8X2, M75.42

**Relations:**
- mapped to → Body Region

**Examples:**
- M54.59 Other Low Back Pain
- M17.2 Bilateral Post-Traumatic Osteoarthritis of Knee
- M75.41 Impingement Syndrome Right Shoulder

### Recommendations / Treatment Plan
Ordered list of clinical recommendations including therapies, procedures, medications, and justifications

**Properties:**
- Treatment Justification: Clinical rationale documenting why a recommended treatment is medically necessary, referencing failed conservative treatments and objective findings
  Examples: Patient underwent 3 months of PT with less than 15% pain relief, Intra-articular injection with less than 20% relief, step-therapy failed
- Functional Goals: Short-term and long-term measurable goals for the patient's recovery and functional improvement
  Examples: Walk 1 additional block, Increase ROM by 5-10 degrees, Improve ADL performance including dressing and bathing

**Examples:**
- Physical Therapy
- Acupuncture
- Medial Branch Block L4-L5/L5-S1
- Bilateral Knee Viscosupplementation

### Signature Block
Attestation section including provider signature, date, and co-signing provider if applicable

**Relations:**
- signed by → Provider

**Examples:**
- James Carter, M.D. (signature)
- Marcus Reid, D.O. (co-signer)

### Radiology Report
Appended or referenced formal imaging report including technique, clinical history, findings, impression, and radiologist signature

**Properties:**
- Radiologist: The physician who interpreted the imaging study, including name, credentials, and signature date
  Examples: Eliana Tan, M.D., Signed 2/12/2024
- Imaging Findings: Detailed radiologic observations at each spinal level or anatomical region with impression summary
  Examples: L4-5: Anterolisthesis, severe bilateral facet arthropathy, moderate to severe canal stenosis, Grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5

**Examples:**
- MRI Lumbar Spine Non-Contrast
- MRI Cervical Spine Non-Contrast

## Dimensions

### Visit Type
Classification of the encounter that drives the scope and structure of the clinical note

### Claim Type
The legal classification of the injury or illness claim

### Body Region
Anatomical area of complaint or examination; drives the structure of the physical exam
